headline: 236/290 maintainer-adjudicated regressions detected at the introducing PR
method: Run the shipped jrs:java-review-agent finders on the diff that INTRODUCED a bug, score recall against the defect the maintainers' later fix PR proves. Finders-only (upper-bound recall). 0 fabrications across all runs. Finder model is per-pair (see finder_model field) — batches 1–7 Opus 4.8, batches 8–22 Grok 4.5 (Grok 4.5 n=220, Opus 4.8 n=70).

Tasks.run() with a non-null service takes runParallel: it submits the single task and then blocks the caller in waitFor(futures), which busy-polls Thread.sleep(10) until completion. So report() still blocks the caller for the full HTTP POST

IoUring multishot cancel clears readPending before re-schedule → permanent stall
FlushRegionProcedure execute/complete race on unsync dispatched/succ
snapshot shared table lock + holdLock deadlocks with Enable exclusive
global queue cleanup helper never wired into completionCleanup → hang
QuotaCache ConcurrentHashMap→HashMap data race
removed synchronized from FlightServerChannel.close() → double-close race
close vs request race on collect sink socket
expire path decrements unacked while redeliver is non-atomic
gRPC @RunOnVirtualThread half-close before message
pruneFinishedQueryInfo sets queryScheduler null without abort → race/leak

pairs.severity_or_note: init commitNeeded; missed getStateStore context wipe
pairs.defect: getStateStore unconditionally resets live ProcessorRecordContext → timestamps wiped
INT96 dict decode setLong(idx) uses row index as byte offset → corruption
Variant decimal shredding hardcodes .length(16) vs decimalRequiredBytes
txn buffer stages all CFs into pendingWrites → offset/Position leaks into data iteration
snapshot restore breaks for index-sort (parent field missing on prune)
epoch strings via strict Long.parseLong wrong codec/parse
row lineage uses fileSequenceNumber not dataSequenceNumber
checkpoint serializes all-null write-metadata → failover data loss
segrep retry leaves replica behind (achieved vs target ckp)
hybrid time-boundary filters not re-optimized after attach
migration cleanup fetches PREV_ROW+MIGRATION but not LOCATION → null location
flatHash equality-delete channel map misaligned with metadata columns
ArrayOrderInlineNull always constructed before maxValue≤1 check; skipped OptionalColumnAtATimeReader bulk fast path
Removed Netty4WriteThrottlingHandler from HTTP pipeline; lost lower-level write throttling (sold-as-faster)
Default schema resolution FIRST_FILE_WINS→UNION_BY_NAME bypassed schema cache on multi-file globs (~34×)
ConsumedSubpartitionContext.build O(n) iterates all partitions on scheduler hot path
collectFetch always waits on reconciliationCheckFuture even when not reconciling
processOneNow linear-scans completion array on hot write path (10-15% drop; sold-as-safer)
Observer poll Math.min with expired updateVoterSet timer → busy-spin Raft client
Per-handler LoggerFactory.getLogger(canonicalName) CPU cost on share-group persister path
Duplicate getTableToExtract lineage call starts extra BQ query job on export read
ConcurrentReferenceHashMap.compute* always locks even for present read-only keys
MapredContext.createDummy on every GenericUDF initialize bloated query compilation
rewriteNoScoring re-enters ConstantScoreQuery.rewrite → exponential nested Boolean rewrite (sold-as-faster)
ByteBufferUtil setAccessible(true) on every newDirectByteBuffer instead of once
Time-series partitioner many fixed slices on high-CPU → scheduling overhead (sold-as-faster; full revert)
ClosingFuture logging/close made closingFutureToString() eager instead of lazy
Pattern Layout stack rewrite made %xEx extended stack rendering far slower
Per-hydrant SpecificSegmentQueryRunner multiplies Thread.setName cost 50–200×
SQL plan history records plans synchronously on H2/Calcite query paths
Account resources API never checks userManagedAccessAllowed (UMA config theater)
FGAP v2 client-scope assign lacks manage-on-scope (CVE-2026-14614)
reject-ropc-grant policy bypass when conditions fail to resolve client (CVE-2026-9792)
Versioned Account API path lacks checkAccountApiEnabled (CVE-2026-7500)
ReferenceCountedOpenSslContext hard-codes startTls=false; SslContextBuilder.startTls(true) no-op
UpdatingEntryProcessor serializes Subject; client can tamper ExpressionEvalContext for GET_DDL
GDS secure download uses weaker isValidateHttpsAuthentication than sibling secure downloads
Config endpoint password URL rebuild drops ACL username → WRONGPASS after topology discovery
AJP_ALLOWED_REQUEST_ATTRIBUTES_PATTERN not applied in setUndertowOptions (config theater)
DPoP jkt uses PublicKey.getEncoded digest instead of JWK SHA-256 thumbprint (RFC 9449)
Lifecycle set/delete used ACLType.ALL; wrong under Ranger
analytics executeWithProfile bypasses SecurityFilter/index permission path
ssl.clientHostnameVerification ignored when ssl.authProvider set (hardcoded false)
createAndAddStreamThread outside changeThreadCount → duplicate StreamThread index
AdaptivePoolingAllocator free/offerToQueue TOCTOU → chunk leak
Early closeAndClearPendingMessages races reconnect/resend of recycled messages
Proxy close during outbound connect leaks connections (port exhaustion)
ForSt close joins shared background threads used by other instances
asyncLoadIndexInput unconditional decRef after remove races re-insert
ReturnChunkEvent static INSTANCE wrong type → JFR class-init deadlock
eagerAttachInitialAsync vs onLayoutChange exclusive claim race
Single esql_external_io pool for drain+parse + POISON readiness → deadlock
parseV1Mappings complete field replacement broke multi-template deep merge
AvroToRowDataConverters microsecond→nano multiplies by 1e6 not 1000
Reversed ByteArray.compare for raw BYTES min/max metadata
NestedField.from copies defaults onto FIXED physical type → castDefault fails for decimals
var-length dictionary validate rejects BIG_DECIMAL (STRING||BYTES only)
CkpMetadata.bootstrap stopped cleaning checkpoint-meta → stale ckp on JM restart
getLastClusterCommit only sees completed CLUSTER; ignores pending clustering
TableMetadataParser double-closes stream in try-with-resources
HiveCatalog.close closes shared FileIO while tables still need it
Hive metastore proxy factory only tries first constructor param type → DLF catalog fail
bulkScore remainder maxScore uses scores[i+1] instead of scores[i+2] when remaining>2
HttpContentDecompressor passes maxAllocation as BrotliDecoder input size not output cap
terms-lookup fetch uses unregistered indices.query.max_clause_count stuck at 1024
Share-group DLQ stamps records with hiResClockMs (nanoTime) so retention deletes immediately
pointsInMemoryBytes required constructorArg breaks parse of older ShardFieldStats
NumericFieldStats.decodeLong only handles width 4/8; HalfFloat etc throw IAE
BooleanFlatteningRewriter flattens must_not of pure must_not collapsing double-negation
zeroTillAligned uses addr%8 as bytes-to-alignment (wrong for non-aligned addresses)
getNumberOfEntries current-ledger branch misses toPosition same ledger as LAC with smaller entry
BitRateUnit refactor dropped * nics.size() from getTotalNicLimitWithConfiguration
ioBuilder branch of newFileIO silently drops storageCredentials
validate-only cluster checks wired into shared validateConfig used by create/update
small-entry ReadResponse path writeBytes copy never rr.release() → ByteBuf leak
REPOSITORY_SYSTEM_CALL marker stamped as RequestTrace tip overwriting Artifact data → CCE
per-datanode getBlock loop ignores loop variable; all replicas report same blockData
multipart stream state.index=1 reset + maxRead not reset → orphaned parts / silent body truncation
group-flush add responses never flush when writeDataToJournal is false → client timeouts
isProxiable wrongly excludes abstract classes with !isAbstract → lazy proxy break
null-discriminator subclass CHECK uses 'is' instead of 'is not' → inverted constraint
DevServices reallyStart stopped putAll into shared configs → dependent services miss prior config

notes: review_grade A/B = defect on the introducing diff's surface, C = cross-file, per evals/mined-pairs-candidates.md. 'deeper-than-fix' unadjudicated findings are deliberately NOT in these headline fields. Mis-graded candidates dropped pre-scoring are not counted. finder_model is the model that ran the finders for that banked HIT/MISS — do not mix models when quoting a single recall rate without disclosing the split.
